summary: Jeff Atwood shares his C# implementation of the ASCII85 encoding algorithm, ported from a C sample referenced on Wikipedia. The implementation is packaged as a VS.NET 2003 solution and includes both encoding and decoding functionality with data validation. He provides a simple API with Encode and Decode methods plus configuration properties for customization. The post includes a test harness demonstrating the algorithm against sample text, though Atwood acknowledges the demo of converting printable text to printable text isn't particularly impressive.
key_points: Provides a complete C# port of ASCII85 encoding/decoding algorithm from Wikipedia's C reference
Implementation includes data validation and configurable options like line length and prefix/suffix marks
Packaged as a VS.NET 2003 solution with test harness for string, binary, and error scenarios
ASCII85 is most useful for binary data encoding, not text-to-text conversion
money_quotes: Of course, converting printable text to, er... printable text... isn't exactly an earth-shattering demo.
Normally you'd be doing this on some kind of real binary data.
key_insight: ASCII85 encoding is a practical algorithm for converting binary data to printable ASCII, and a clean C# implementation can be built by porting existing C reference code.
